cmd/sink-worker.purpose: Binary entry point for the Kafka-to-ClickHouse sink worker: reads raw ingest CloudEvents from Kafka, deduplicates them, batch-inserts into ClickHouse via streaming.Connector, then publishes ingest notification events to Watermill so the balance-worker can recalculate entitlements. Contains only wiring and startup — all sink logic lives in openmeter/sink.

patterns.name: Wire-generated DI via initializeApplication
patterns.description: wire.go declares the Application struct and wire.Build call; wire_gen.go is generated and must never be hand-edited. Add new dependencies by adding a field to Application and a provider to wire.Build.

patterns.name: oklog/run group for actor lifecycle
patterns.description: main.go adds exactly three actors to run.Group: app.Sink.Run/Close, TelemetryServer.ListenAndServe/Shutdown, and run.SignalHandler. New background goroutines must be added as group.Add pairs, not standalone goroutines.
patterns.example: group.Add(func() error { return app.Sink.Run(ctx) }, func(err error) { _ = app.Sink.Close() })

patterns.name: Viper + pflag config loading with conf.Validate()
patterns.description: Config is loaded via viper.Unmarshal into config.Configuration then validated with conf.Validate() before Wire initialization. Never skip validation or read config after initializeApplication.
patterns.example: v.Unmarshal(&conf); conf.Validate(); app, cleanup, err := initializeApplication(ctx, conf)

patterns.name: Metadata via common.NewMetadata with binary name
patterns.description: The local metadata() function calls common.NewMetadata(conf, version, "sink-worker") to tag telemetry and logs with the binary identity. Every cmd binary must provide this function.
patterns.example: func metadata(conf config.Configuration) common.Metadata { return common.NewMetadata(conf, version, "sink-worker") }

patterns.name: All providers sourced from app/common, not local
patterns.description: wire.Build references only common.* provider sets and the local metadata/NewLogger functions. No domain logic or direct service construction belongs in wire.go.

patterns.name: run.WithReverseShutdownOrder for graceful shutdown
patterns.description: group.Run(run.WithReverseShutdownOrder()) ensures Sink shuts down before telemetry server, matching the add order. Do not call group.Run() without this option.

key_file_guides.role: Declares Application struct and wire.Build provider list. This is the DI spec — only edited when adding/removing app-level dependencies.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Build tag `//go:build wireinject` must be present; omitting it causes duplicate symbol errors with wire_gen.go. Never add business logic here.

key_file_guides.role: Generated by `go generate ./...` (Wire). Contains the actual initializeApplication implementation with ordered cleanup chain.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Never hand-edit. Cleanup functions are chained in reverse-construction order — manual edits break the cleanup chain silently.

key_file_guides.role: Entry point: config loading, Wire init, run group assembly, signal handling. Contains no domain logic.
key_file_guides.watch_for: app.SetGlobals() must be called before any logging to initialize OTel globals. The cleanup defer must remain unconditional even when initializeApplication errors.

key_file_guides.role: Provides version/revision/revisionDate globals populated by ldflags and debug.BuildInfo. Shared pattern across all cmd binaries.
key_file_guides.watch_for: The `//nolint:gochecknoglobals` and `//nolint:gochecknoinits` suppressions are intentional — do not remove them.

anti_patterns: Adding business logic or domain service calls directly in main.go or wire.go — all logic belongs in openmeter/sink or app/common
Hand-editing wire_gen.go — it is fully generated and any manual change will be overwritten by `go generate`
Introducing a new goroutine outside of run.Group — bypasses graceful shutdown and signal handling
Constructing domain services (e.g. sink.Sink) directly in wire.go instead of delegating to common.Sink provider set
Calling context.Background() for the run context — the root context is created in main() and passed through; use it

decisions.decision: WatermillNoPublisher is used instead of a full Watermill publisher
decisions.rationale: The sink worker only publishes ingest flush events via a dedicated Kafka publisher (NewSinkWorkerPublisher); it does not need the full system-events Watermill router, so WatermillNoPublisher keeps the wiring minimal.
GlobalInitializer.SetGlobals() initializes OTel global meter/tracer/propagator in one call, ensuring all downstream packages that rely on otel globals are consistently configured.

decisions.decision: Sink and TelemetryServer are the only two long-running actors
decisions.rationale: Sink worker has a single responsibility (Kafka → ClickHouse). The telemetry server is a mandatory sidecar for health/metrics. Keeping the run group minimal reduces shutdown complexity.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new background actor (e.g. a reconciler) to the sink worker
